headline: Nvidia offers startups compute-for-equity deals
detail: Nvidia trading compute power for revenue share signals it is aggressively seeding the next layer of AI infrastructure. This keeps the AI buildout narrative alive and is directly positive sentiment for chip names and semi ETFs like SMH and SOXL. Not a same-day price mover but reinforces the bull case for your core holdings.
rec: No action today - this is a slow-burn positive for SMH and SOXL, not an entry trigger. If SMH stabilizes and SOXL holds above $200, that context supports holding rather than cutting, but don't add on this alone.

headline: OpenAI proposes 5% stake to Trump administration
detail: OpenAI trying to buy political goodwill reduces near-term regulatory risk for the AI ecosystem broadly. Less Washington friction is a soft positive for AI-exposed names across your book - CRWD, and indirectly SMH. It does not change fundamentals but removes a headline risk.
rec: No specific trade here - this is noise reduction, not a catalyst. Hold CRWD as is; stop at $590 is far away and your 7.4% gain gives you room.

headline: Google loses final appeal on $4.7B EU antitrust fine
detail: Confirms EU will keep pressing Big Tech hard on antitrust; this is a slow-moving negative for large-cap tech sentiment in Europe. Your QQQ and SPY have Google exposure, but the headline is old news - market already largely priced this. Minimal fresh impact.
rec: Nothing to do here for your book. QQQ and SPY stops ($670 and $705) are well below current prices; sit tight.

headline: Warsh comment injects life into debasement trade
detail: Any Fed-credibility wobble or dollar-debasement fear can briefly pressure growth and tech multiples, which is exactly your concentrated exposure. It was a one-day move per the headline itself, so sustained damage is unlikely - but worth knowing this risk channel exists for your margin book.
rec: No trade, but keep it in mind. If dollar-debasement talk accelerates and rates spike, your leveraged names (SOXL, SPAL) get hit first and hardest - that would be a reason to cut early rather than wait for stops.
